seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Farnham and Farrants Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Farnham vs Farrants Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Farnham and Farrants Hill.
heading: Farnham vs Farrants Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Farnham, New South Wales and Farrants Hill, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Farnham has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Farrants Hill (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Farnham or Farrants Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Farnham has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Farrants Hill scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Farnham scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Farrants Hill scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Farnham or Farrants Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Farnham has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.77% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
